The chat segment contains a single message from user resethill in the elizaos Discord channel. No further discussion, solutions, or implementations were provided in this limited chat segment.

The chat segment is extremely brief, containing only two messages. One user (casper) is expressing frustration about waiting for a week for something unspecified. Another user (fen sama) is thanking someone for fixing an issue related to "$quaill" and describing a problem with token purchases after migration, where they received fewer tokens than expected due to liquidity pool issues.

# Analysis of 🥇-partners Discord Channel

## 1. Summary
The chat segment contains a brief discussion about Solana permissioned blockchains (appchains). DorianD shared a Helius blog post about this technology and suggested it could be used for auto.fun projects. The proposal involves initially deploying projects on these appchains to save costs and allow the team to run their own validators. Once projects reach 100% of their bonding curve, they could be migrated to Solana mainnet. DorianD noted potential challenges with moving SOL tokens to the appchain for funding, suggesting stablecoins as an alternative. Odilitime responded that while they have infrastructure to run Solana nodes, the decision isn't theirs to make. The conversation was brief and exploratory rather than conclusive.

## 2. FAQ
Q: What happened this time? (asked by CtrlAltElite) A: Unanswered

## 3. Help Interactions
Helper: yikesawjeez | Helpee: Patt | Context: Patt mentioned needing to look into something (context unclear) | Resolution: yikesawjeez offered to either set it up or provide guidance for a fee/revenue share if it's for a product

## 4. Action Items
Type: Technical | Description: Explore using Solana permissioned blockchains (appchains) for auto.fun projects before migrating to mainnet | Mentioned By: DorianD
Type: Technical | Description: Investigate solutions for moving SOL tokens to appchains or using stablecoins instead | Mentioned By: DorianD
